locked_parameters.data_and_localization_convention: W_i=(Y_i,D_i,X_i) are iid, D_i\in\{0,1\}, x_0 is fixed, K_{h_n} is a nonnegative localizer, p_n=E_P K_{h_n}(X), and \ell_n(x)=K_{h_n}(x)/p_n
locked_parameters.identifying_convention: the causal estimand is \tau_P(x_0)=E_P[Y(1)-Y(0)\mid X=x_0] under consistency, conditional unconfoundedness, and overlap e_P(X)\in[\epsilon,1-\epsilon]
locked_parameters.model_restrictions: the model \mathcal{P}_n^{\mathrm{SA\text{-}CATE}} is the class of laws satisfying Assumptions~\ref{ass:causal}--\ref{ass:local-rich}: local bias bounded by b_n, local effective sample size n p_n\to\infty, and black-box local nuisance rates r_{g,n},r_{m,n} measured in the \ell_n-weighted local L_2(P) norm
locked_parameters.estimator_convention: the estimator \hat\theta_n^{\mathrm{locDR}} is the K-fold cross-fit localized doubly robust learner defined in the formal setup

named_assumptions.label: ass:causal
named_assumptions.statement: The observations W_i=(Y_i,D_i,X_i) are iid. Consistency holds, (Y(1),Y(0))\perp D\mid X, and there exists \epsilon\in(0,1/2) such that \epsilon\le e(X)\le 1-\epsilon almost surely. The point x_0 lies in the support of X in the sense that p_n=E_P[K_{h_n}(X)]>0 for all large n.

named_assumptions.label: ass:bounded
named_assumptions.statement: There exists M<\infty such that |Y|\le M almost surely and |g_a(X)|\le M almost surely for a\in\{0,1\}. For each bandwidth value h under consideration, the localizer satisfies 0\le K_h(X)\le 1, p_h=E_P[K_h(X)]>0, and its normalized weights obey E_P[\ell_h(X)^2]\asymp p_h^{-1} uniformly over P\in\mathcal{P}_n^{\mathrm{SA\text{-}CATE}}.

named_assumptions.label: ass:bias
named_assumptions.statement: For the chosen bandwidth sequence h_n, the smoothed target obeys |\theta_{h_n}(P)-\tau_P(x_0)|\le b_n uniformly over P\in\mathcal{P}_n^{\mathrm{SA\text{-}CATE}}.

named_assumptions.label: ass:nuisance
named_assumptions.statement: For the fold-specific nuisance estimators used in \hat\theta_n^{\mathrm{locDR}}, R_{g,n}^2:=E_P[\ell_{h_n}(X)\{(\hat g_1-g_1)^2+(\hat g_0-g_0)^2\}]=O_P(r_{g,n}) and R_{m,n}^2:=E_P[\ell_{h_n}(X)(\hat e-e)^2]=O_P(r_{m,n}), with r_{g,n}\to0 and r_{m,n}\to0.

named_assumptions.label: ass:effective
named_assumptions.statement: The bandwidth sequence is such that n p_n\to\infty, while p_n\to0 is allowed. The number of folds K is fixed, and the fold sizes satisfy |I_k|/n\to\pi_k\in(0,1), so each evaluation fold and its training complement are asymptotically nondegenerate.

named_assumptions.label: ass:local-rich
named_assumptions.statement: For every large n, the model \mathcal{P}_n^{\mathrm{SA\text{-}CATE}}(x_0,h_n,r_{g,n},r_{m,n},b_n,\epsilon) contains bounded local perturbation pairs supported inside the effective support of K_{h_n} that can vary, separately or jointly, the point target, the local mass p_n, and the nuisance components (g_0,g_1,e) while preserving Assumptions~\ref{ass:causal}--\ref{ass:effective}. These perturbations are the admissible two-point experiments for the later Le Cam converse.

conjectures.label: upper
conjectures.statement: Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:causal}--\ref{ass:effective}, the estimator \hat\theta_n^{\mathrm{locDR}} satisfies \[ \left|\hat\theta_n^{\mathrm{locDR}}-\tau_P(x_0)\right| =O_P\!\left(b_n+(n p_n)^{-1/2}+\sqrt{r_{g,n}r_{m,n}}\right) \] uniformly over \mathcal{P}_n^{\mathrm{SA\text{-}CATE}}.

method_exemplar.carried_over: DR pseudo-outcome skeleton, adapted to normalized point localization and black-box local nuisance rates.

conjectures.label: lower
conjectures.statement: Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:causal}--\ref{ass:local-rich}, there exists a constant c>0 such that every estimator \tilde\theta_n obeys \[ \sup_{P\in\mathcal{P}_n^{\mathrm{SA\text{-}CATE}}} E_P\left|\tilde\theta_n-\tau_P(x_0)\right| \ge c\left(b_n+(n p_n)^{-1/2}+\sqrt{r_{g,n}r_{m,n}}\right). \]

method_exemplar.carried_over: Structure-agnostic perturbation and Le Cam lower-bound template, specialized to a localized CATE point target.

secondary_method_exemplars.carried_over: Treatment-effect lower-bound channel for black-box nuisance rates.
Localized CATE geometry and the role of shrinking neighborhood mass.

supporting_theorems.label: localized-aipw
supporting_theorems.statement: Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:causal} and~\ref{ass:bounded}, the localized AIPW score satisfies \[ E_P[\psi_h(W;\eta_P)]=\theta_h(P). \] For any nuisance tuple \tilde\eta=(\tilde g_0,\tilde g_1,\tilde e) with \tilde e\in[\epsilon/2,1-\epsilon/2], the standard product drift obeys \[ \left|E_P[\psi_h(W;\tilde\eta)]-\theta_h(P)\right| \le C_\epsilon \left(E_P[\ell_h(X)\{(\tilde g_1-g_1)^2+(\tilde g_0-g_0)^2\}]\right)^{1/2} \left(E_P[\ell_h(X)(\tilde e-e)^2]\right)^{1/2}. \]
supporting_theorems.proof_tex:
\begin{theorem}[Theorem 1: localized AIPW identity]\label{thm:localized-aipw}
Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:causal} and~\ref{ass:bounded}, the localized AIPW score satisfies
\[
  E_P[\psi_h(W;\eta_P)]=\theta_h(P).
\]
For any nuisance tuple \(\tilde\eta=(\tilde g_0,\tilde g_1,\tilde e)\) with \(\tilde e\in[\epsilon/2,1-\epsilon/2]\), the standard product drift obeys
\[
  \left|E_P[\psi_h(W;\tilde\eta)]-\theta_h(P)\right|
  \le C_\epsilon
  \left(E_P[\ell_h(X)\{(\tilde g_1-g_1)^2+(\tilde g_0-g_0)^2\}]\right)^{1/2}
  \left(E_P[\ell_h(X)(\tilde e-e)^2]\right)^{1/2}.
\]
\end{theorem}
\begin{proof}
Write \(\eta_P=(g_0,g_1,e)\). Conditioning on \(X\), using Assumption~\ref{ass:causal}, and recalling that \(E[Y\mid D=a,X]=g_a(X)\), we obtain
\[
\begin{aligned}
E_P[\psi_h(W;\eta_P)\mid X]
&=\ell_h(X)\left\{g_1(X)-g_0(X)
+\frac{E_P[D\{Y-g_1(X)\}\mid X]}{e(X)}
-\frac{E_P[(1-D)\{Y-g_0(X)\}\mid X]}{1-e(X)}\right\} \\
&=\ell_h(X)\{g_1(X)-g_0(X)\}
=\ell_h(X)\tau_P(X).
\end{aligned}
\]
Taking expectations gives \(E_P[\psi_h(W;\eta_P)]=E_P[\ell_h(X)\tau_P(X)]=\theta_h(P)\).

For the drift calculation, let \(\Delta_a=\tilde g_a-g_a\) for \(a=0,1\) and \(\Delta_e=\tilde e-e\). A second conditioning-on-\(X\) calculation yields
\[
\begin{aligned}
E_P[\psi_h(W;\tilde\eta)\mid X]-\ell_h(X)\tau_P(X)
&=\ell_h(X)\left\{\Delta_1(X)+\frac{e(X)(g_1(X)-\tilde g_1(X))}{\tilde e(X)}
-\Delta_0(X)-\frac{(1-e(X))(g_0(X)-\tilde g_0(X))}{1-\tilde e(X)}\right\} \\
&=\ell_h(X)\Delta_e(X)
\left\{\frac{\Delta_1(X)}{\tilde e(X)}+\frac{\Delta_0(X)}{1-\tilde e(X)}\right\}.
\end{aligned}
\]
The last identity is obtained by regrouping the treated and control terms separately:
\(\Delta_1-e\Delta_1/\tilde e=\Delta_e\Delta_1/\tilde e\) and
\(-\Delta_0+(1-e)\Delta_0/(1-\tilde e)=\Delta_e\Delta_0/(1-\tilde e)\).
Since \(\tilde e\in[\epsilon/2,1-\epsilon/2]\), both denominators are bounded below by \(\epsilon/2\), so for a constant \(C_\epsilon<\infty\) depending only on \(\epsilon\),
\[
\left|E_P[\psi_h(W;\tilde\eta)]-\theta_h(P)\right|
\le C_\epsilon E_P\!\left[\ell_h(X)|\Delta_e(X)|\{ |\Delta_1(X)|+|\Delta_0(X)|\}\right].
\]
Applying Cauchy--Schwarz under the weighted measure with density \(\ell_h(X)\) relative to \(P\), and then using \((a+b)^2\le 2(a^2+b^2)\), gives
\[
\left|E_P[\psi_h(W;\tilde\eta)]-\theta_h(P)\right|
\le C_\epsilon
\left(E_P[\ell_h(X)(\tilde e-e)^2]\right)^{1/2}
\left(E_P[\ell_h(X)\{(\tilde g_1-g_1)^2+(\tilde g_0-g_0)^2\}]\right)^{1/2},
\]
which is the claimed product drift bound.
\end{proof}

substrate_survey.note: Existing CATE setup abstractions appear to match the target estimand level.
The rate statement aligns with existing structure-agnostic optimality primitives.
Sample-splitting primitives already exist at a compatible abstraction.
No direct local-search hit was needed for the stage-0.0 math note; exact substrate mapping is deferred to Stage 1 once the theorem statements are fixed.
